pitch_me_signals.title: Investors Open to Pitches

new_funds.title: Recent Fund Announcements

signals.thesis: Fintech Plaid Completes Tender Offer At $8B Valuation
Left General Catalyst to found Verdict Capital as Founder & Managing Director (2 months in role). New fund launch — potential new investor for KTLYST.
Now Founding Partner at Silver Buckshot Ventures. Former NYT cybersecurity reporter turned VC — deeply connected in cybersecurity, potential investor for KTLYST.
Boldstart Fund VII ($250M) announcement on LinkedIn - targeting AI founders at early/embryonic stages for autonomous enterprise

Boldstart Ventures Fund VII announced - $250M AI fund targeting early/embryonic stage founders building autonomous enterprise. Also celebrated Protect AI acquisition by Palo Alto Networks at RSAC2025.
Bessemer invested in Upwind Security - $250M Series B at $1.5B valuation for cloud security startup
Claroty raised $150M+ at $3B valuation - third mega round from Team8 portfolio (following Port.io $100M and Classiq $130M)

daily.executive_summary: Market sentiment shows investor skepticism toward generic AI-SaaS and agentic security tools, which creates a favorable opening for KTLYST's governance-first, infrastructure-layer thesis. The cybersecurity sector is experiencing a re-evaluation ($20B+ valuation corrections cited), indicating VCs are moving away from narrow point solutions toward platforms that solve institutional learning and decision durability—exactly KTLYST's positioning as the learning control plane above fragmented execution tools.
key_themes: VCs rejecting generic AI-SaaS without clear infrastructure/ROI differentiation
Cybersecurity market correction and repricing—opportunity for differentiated infrastructure plays
Growing recognition that AI/automation risks require governance, provenance, and auditable decision-making
Shift from point-solution SOC tooling toward horizontal, cross-tool coordination layers
Institutional memory and compounding security learning as competitive moat
recommended_actions: Monitor TechCrunch Venture and Venture Beat cybersecurity coverage daily—map which VCs are quoted rejecting agentic/generic AI SaaS to identify firms open to governance-first infrastructure pitches

Research investors quoted in '20VC x SaaStr This Week' cybersecurity segment ($20B correction story)—they're actively evaluating security infrastructure repricing and may be scouting next-generation control-plane plays

Prepare pitch positioning that explicitly contrasts KTLYST with agentic SOC automation and detection-as-code trends; emphasize governance, provenance, and institutional learning as the underinvested layer
Track security-focused micro-VCs and seed funds (look for recent announcements in Q1 2026) to identify newly capitalized early-stage investors with fresh deployment capital for infrastructure

Create a tracker of VCs who've invested in horizontal platform layers (e.g., orchestration, data governance, compliance infrastructure) outside pure security—map potential cross-vertical thesis overlap
Develop a 'What We're Not' one-pager (not detection-as-code, not SOC automation, not agentic) for outreach—use investor skepticism toward those categories as an opening

weekly.executive_summary: This week reveals a critical market divergence: investors are explicitly rejecting generic 'AI for security' and SOC automation plays, while simultaneously signaling appetite for governance, infrastructure, and institutional memory solutions. The cybersecurity market saw a $20B valuation reset (Anthropic impact), but the underlying trend favors horizontal infrastructure layers—exactly KTLYST's positioning. Enterprise MCP adoption outpacing security controls is a direct validation of the control-plane thesis.
key_themes: AI skepticism in security SaaS—investors want infrastructure, not agents or alert-fatigue tools
Control plane and governance architecture gaining investor interest as security matures
Enterprise MCP adoption crisis exposing gaps in security orchestration and institutional learning
Security as data/intelligence systems narrative strengthening; compounding institutional memory becoming defensibility signal
Detection engineering and prevention engineering shifting from point tools to governance layers
Cybersecurity valuation reset but infrastructure plays remain resilient

hot_investors.name: Unknown
hot_investors.reason: VentureBeat signal on MCP adoption outpacing security controls is a direct validation of KTLYST's core insight: execution tools proliferate, but governance layer is missing. Investors backing horizontal orchestration/control-plane plays will see this as proof of market need.
hot_investors.recent_activity: Market data showing enterprise MCP sprawl creating governance vacuum—signals investor interest in control-plane solutions

recommended_actions: Immediately anchor pitch narrative to 'investors reject AI-wrapper security; demand governance infrastructure' trend from TechCrunch/SaaStr signals—position KTLYST as the compounding institutional memory layer, not an AI agent or detection tool

Weaponize the 'Enterprise MCP adoption outpacing security controls' signal in outreach: it's proof that execution tools scale faster than governance, creating wedge for SLCP. Use this as qualification filter in investor meetings.

Map the SaaStr/$20B cybersecurity reset: identify which early-stage/seed VCs are actively re-deploying capital away from consumer security AI and into B2B infrastructure plays. These are warm leads for KTLYST's Series A/B trajectory.

Research which VCs funded Anthropic, Crunchbase-tracked security acquisitions (Arctic Wolf/Sevco), and governance-infrastructure plays—they've shown conviction in category and will have dry powder post-correction.

Create 1-pager titled 'Why Detection Tools Fail: The Missing Learning Layer' using MCP/control-plane data to position KTLYST as infrastructure, not tooling. Share with security-focused LPs before formal pitch.

Monitor detection engineering + prevention engineering discourse in SaaStr, 20VC, Insight Partners circles—early signal that these will be 2026 funding categories; KTLYST is the learning infrastructure enabling both.
Deprioritize outreach to generalist AI/SaaS VCs; focus exclusively on security-native, infrastructure-focused, and governance-compliance investors who understand horizontal platform dynamics.

Flag that no explicit 'pitch-me' signals from investors this week—suggest attending SaaStr, 20VC events or security-focused founder networks where infrastructure investors congregate post-AI correction.
